_note:
Phase-1 research output for the 2026-07-23 Anglo-accessibility gap-analysis re-pass (hesder yeshivot / mechinot / Anglo women's gap-year seminaries) -- REPLACES the earlier same-day candidate-programs.json produced before the Anglo-accessibility bar was made explicit. NOT YET IMPORTED -- this is research only, no DB writes were made. Do not run prisma/import-researched.ts against this file without a human review pass first (see per-candidate adminNote for UNCERTAIN Anglo-accessibility calls and unresolved data-quality flags).

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: earlier today, commit 9d2cd22 imported 5 programs (3 hesder yeshivot: Yeshivat Heichal Eliyahu, Yeshivat Lev Tel Aviv, Yeshivat Nahariya; 2 mechinot: Mechinat Ateret Yerushalayim, Mechinat Maamakim) from the prior candidate-programs.json under an 'Anglo gap' research task, even though that pass's own research notes admitted none of the 5 had confirmed Anglo-accessibility (3 were noted 'Hebrew-only, no dedicated overseas/English program'; 2 had gender/audience/Anglo-accessibility explicitly 'not independently confirmed'). Those 5 are now live in the DB regardless -- they are NOT counted as gap-filling Anglo candidates in this pass, and this pass's researchers were told not to re-propose them (they didn't).

DB coverage going into this pass was already extensive and was independently verified against the live DB (not just the prior pass's word for it): 60 published hesder-tagged, 90 published mechina-tagged, and 67 published girls-only+gap-year-tagged programs, a large fraction of each already carrying an Anglo-accessibility signal (overseas-program/lone-soldier tag, or ENGLISH/BOTH websiteLanguage). Three parallel researchers each cross-referenced the full existing list by website domain (not just name spelling, which varies a lot in Hebrew-to-English transliteration) before proposing anything.

One candidate the mechinot researcher initially proposed (Hevruta Gap-Year Program / Mechinat Chevruta, Shalom Hartman Institute) was caught in a post-research verification pass as ALREADY existing in the DB (slug hevruta-gap-year-program, status PUBLISHED, contactWebsite hartman.org.il) -- it didn't show up in the tag-filtered existing-programs list handed to that researcher because the existing DB row isn't tagged mechina/pre-army/yeshiva (tags: age-gap-year, scholarships-available, coed, jerusalem, flexible-religously, integration-medium). This is a real duplicate, not a new candidate, and is excluded here. Flagging as a DATA QUALITY NOTE for a separate pass: the existing DB row for this program may be under-tagged relative to what it actually is (a Hartman-run pre-military-adjacent gap-year program) -- worth a moderator look, but out of scope to fix here since this task is research-only and does not write to the DB.

Both remaining candidates in gap_year_seminaries_women were independently re-verified against the FULL DB by name and domain (not just the girls-only+gap-year tag-filtered list) and confirmed genuinely absent.

Tags given below are exact Tag.name strings (not slugs), for resolveTagsByName -- every tag used across all three researchers came from the existing vocabulary; ZERO new tags were proposed or needed for this batch. contactEmail was never populated for any candidate (none observed on an official page) per lib/importGuards.ts. Extra fields beyond the ResearchedProgram type (hebrewName, goodFor, hasScholarship, hasCollegeCredit, angloAccessibility) are carried for the human review pass and the chat report, and will be ignored by import-researched.ts itself if this file is ever imported as-is -- carry the confirmed ones into good-for.json / facet-tags-by-slug.json by hand in a future enrichment pass, same as prior batches.

hesder_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Siach Yitzchak
hesder_yeshivot.description: Yeshivat Siach Yitzchak is a hesder yeshiva in Efrat, in the Gush Etzion region, combining intensive Torah study with IDF service. Founded in 1996 as a beit midrash by Rabbi Shagar and Rabbi Yair Dreyfus and joining the hesder track in 2001, the yeshiva is known for its emphasis on open, searching engagement with Torah alongside Jewish thought and philosophy. Alongside its core hesder track, the yeshiva also runs programs for older students and married avreichim who wish to continue studying in its distinctive style.
hesder_yeshivot.goodFor: Religious Zionist young men entering the hesder track who are drawn to an open, philosophically engaged style of Torah learning alongside military service.
hesder_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva via the contact form, email, phone, or WhatsApp listed on its website.
hesder_yeshivot.adminNote: Not a duplicate: absent from the DB's 60 existing hesder-tagged programs (checked by domain siach.org.il and by name/location, no match) and independently re-checked against the full DB by name/domain with zero matches. Confirmed as a genuine hesder yeshiva via Hebrew Wikipedia (joined the hesder track in 2001). Has its own independent, own-hosted official website with a real (non-machine-translated) English page at /en/ -- genuine evidence of intentional Anglo-facing content -- but no explicit statement found anywhere (English or Hebrew page) of an overseas-student track, lone-soldier population, or American/English-speaking cohort, so marked UNCERTAIN rather than CONFIRMED on Anglo-accessibility; a moderator with more direct knowledge of this yeshiva's actual student body should make the final call. 'Yehuda desert' location tag matches this DB's existing convention for other Gush Etzion-area programs (Bat Ayin Yeshiva, Yeshivat Darchei Yonatan, Yeshivat Drisha, Migdal Oz are all tagged yehuda-desert despite being hill country, not literal desert) -- verified against those precedents, not a guess. Phone, cost, and exact program-year duration were not found on the org's own site and are left blank/UNVERIFIED rather than guessed. Sources: https://siach.org.il/, https://siach.org.il/en/, https://he.wikipedia.org/wiki/ישיבת_שיח_יצחק.

gap_year_seminaries_women.name: Pninim Seminary
gap_year_seminaries_women.description: Pninim Seminary is a Bais Yaakov-style post-high-school seminary in the Har Nof neighborhood of Jerusalem, offering discussion-based classes in Tanach, Jewish thought, hashkafa, and practical Jewish living alongside individual mentorship. Students receive personalized guidance from a dedicated faculty and take part in Eretz Yisrael experiential programming, including a trip to Poland. The program has run for over two decades and has graduated more than 1,300 alumnae.
gap_year_seminaries_women.goodFor: Bais Yaakov high school graduates seeking a warm, mentorship-focused seminary year with a strong hashkafa and personal-growth emphasis.
gap_year_seminaries_women.signupInstructions: Contact the office by phone or email; a contact form is available on the official site.
gap_year_seminaries_women.adminNote: Not a duplicate: independently re-verified against the FULL DB (all 67 existing girls-only+gap-year programs plus a whole-DB name/domain search) with zero matches for 'Pninim'/'Neimas'/'Neimos' or the pninimseminary.com domain. Independently hosted official site confirmed live via direct fetch. Anglo/American targeting confirmed via a dedicated USA contact number (203 area code, Connecticut) and a separate USA financial office in addition to the Israel office -- a strong, concrete signal of real American enrollment/fundraising infrastructure, not just theoretical openness to foreigners. Possible duplicate-risk alias to double-check before publishing: Touro University's seminary list and a GuideStar nonprofit filing both reference 'Neimas/Neimos (Peninim) Bais Yaakov' -- likely the same institution's US-side nonprofit under a historical name, but the identity chain was not 100% confirmed; re-check before import in case it's actually a second, related-but-distinct entity. No tuition figure, Hebrew name, or specific alumnae-hometown breakdown was found on the pages reviewed (home + contact + about only) -- left blank/UNVERIFIED rather than guessed. Sources: https://pninimseminary.com/, https://pninimseminary.com/contact, https://pninimseminary.com/about/, https://lcw.touro.edu/admissions--aid/israel-option/participating-seminaries/.

gap_year_seminaries_women.name: Kitov Sachra Seminary
gap_year_seminaries_women.description: Kitov Sachra Seminary is a Bais Yaakov institution in Jerusalem offering a post-high-school seminary program for single women as well as a separate program for married women, with an emphasis on daily classes, mentor meetings, and trips.
gap_year_seminaries_women.goodFor: Bais Yaakov graduates, and separately married women, seeking a seminary program in Jerusalem described by the institution itself as fully funded.
gap_year_seminaries_women.cost: UNVERIFIED/conflicting -- the seminary's own site states the program is fully funded with no tuition, but a third-party listing (Masa Israel) prices it at roughly $32,000 before Masa grants/scholarships. Unresolved; do not publish a cost figure without direct confirmation from the institution.
gap_year_seminaries_women.signupInstructions: Apply via the 'Apply' link on the official site.
gap_year_seminaries_women.adminNote: Not a duplicate: independently re-verified against the FULL DB (all 67 existing girls-only+gap-year programs plus a whole-DB name/domain search) with zero matches for 'Kitov'/'Sachra'/'Ki Tov' or the kitovseminary.com domain. Institution is real and appears consistently across multiple independent sources (Masa Israel, Touro's participating-seminaries list, Jeff Seidel's Travel Guide, a Yeshiva World forum discussion), all naming the same Jerusalem/Geula address -- confirms a genuine, currently-operating institution. HOWEVER the only website found (kitovseminary.com) is built on the no-code tool 'Lovable' and its raw HTML still carries the default placeholder title 'Lovable App' / description 'Kitov Brilliance Hub' -- suggests this may be an unofficial or unfinished site (possibly made by a parent, alumna, or contractor) rather than a polished institution-maintained page, and kitovseminary.org (referenced in the site's own contact info) doesn't resolve via DNS. Combined with the tuition discrepancy above, marked UNCERTAIN on Anglo-accessibility/site-legitimacy pending direct confirmation the site is sanctioned by the seminary itself -- do not treat contactWebsite here as fully authoritative without that confirmation. No Hebrew name or alumnae figures found. Sources: https://kitovseminary.com/, https://www.masaisrael.org/program/kitov-sachra-seminary/, https://lcw.touro.edu/admissions--aid/israel-option/participating-seminaries/, https://jeffseidel.com/jeffstravelguide/listings/ki-tov-sachra/.
